content: With all the capabilities with TARA what do u think are we missing to become the best assistant my long term long is to make you TARA something like Jarvis of Iron man something great -- Can u check all the function calls and everything and tell me what are we even missing?

content.text:
Love the vision. Here’s a crisp audit of TARA’s current stack and what’s missing to feel “Jarvis-level”.

What you already do well
- Solid Google Workspace coverage: Gmail, Calendar, Docs, Drive, Meet, Sheets, Tasks, Contacts. This enables scheduling, email triage, meeting setup, basic knowledge work, and task capture inside one suite.

Biggest capability gaps (what’s missing)
1) Multimodal, real-time IO
- Always-on voice (wake word), high-quality TTS/STT, call-in/call-out, meeting live co-pilot, screen understanding (read what’s on-screen), image/PDF OCR and understanding.

2) Cross-channel communications beyond Google
- Slack/Teams, WhatsApp/Telegram/SMS/iMessage, Zoom/Webex. People don’t live in one channel; TARA must follow context across them.

3) Memory, personalization, and long-horizon context
- Durable, editable user memory (preferences, recurring routines, contacts enrichment), multi-session recall, user style/tone, working hours, travel/food/location prefs.

4) Proactive and background autonomy
- Event-driven triggers (time, geo, inbox patterns), watchers (e.g., “monitor this thread and remind me if no reply in 48h”), scheduled jobs, retries, approval gates, notifications.

5) Knowledge and retrieval
- Enterprise/Personal search across Drive, Gmail, Notion/Confluence/Jira/GitHub, with citations; vector indexes; “grounded” answers with source links; policy-based access.

6) OS and device control
- Desktop/mobile automation (open apps, control UI, clipboard, files), RPA-style visual actions, notifications, file watchers.

7) Line-of-business and dev tool integrations
- Project/issue trackers (Jira, Asana, Trello, Linear), docs/wikis (Notion, Confluence), code (GitHub/GitLab), paging (Opsgenie/PagerDuty), BI/DB connectors (Snowflake/BigQuery/Postgres), CRM (HubSpot/Salesforce).

8) Transactional abilities (India-first)
- UPI payments, expenses and receipts, travel/commute/ordering: Maps/ETA, Ola/Uber/Rapido, Swiggy/Zomato, IRCTC/redBus/KSRTC, MakeMyTrip/Cleartrip, bill pay.

9) Safety, permissions, and governance
- Per-scope consent, dry-run mode, human-in-the-loop approvals for risky actions, audit logs, role-based access, DLP/PII redaction, secrets management, rate-limit/backoff.

10) Observability and reliability
- Tool health monitoring, retries/idempotency, canaries, usage analytics, error dashboards, test harnesses and simulators.

Concrete function/API gaps to close
- Memory: memory_set, memory_get, memory_list, memory_delete
- Web and data: http_request (generic), web_fetch_with_login, file_ocr, pdf_split_merge, image_analyze, file_convert
- Communications: slack_post/list/read/reply, teams_send, whatsapp_send, telegram_send, sms_send/receive, phone_call_start/end
- Meetings: meeting_transcribe_start/stop, meeting_summarize, calendar_smart_schedule (find best slot with constraints)
- Retrieval: knowledge_index_create/update, knowledge_search(query, top_k, sources[]), cite_with_source
- Automation: automation_create_trigger(event, filter, action[]), automation_list/delete, webhook_register, notifications_subscribe
- OS/RPA: os_open_app, os_keystroke, os_click, fs_read/write/list, screenshot_capture
- Work tools: jira_create/update/search, asana_task_create/update, notion_page_read/write, github_issue/pr/create/comment
- India-specific transactions: payments_upi_initiate/verify, maps_get_eta, ride_request_ola/uber, food_order_swiggy/zomato, travel_search_book_irctc/flights/hotels
- Security: approvals_create(flow, risk_level), policy_enforce(action, scopes), secrets_store/get, audit_log_write

High-impact v1.0 additions (order of operations)
1) Durable memory + approvals + audit log (foundation for trust and personalization).
2) Retrieval across Google Drive/Gmail with citations, plus OCR for PDFs/images.
3) Cross-channel comms: Slack + WhatsApp + SMS (send/read/reply + thread linkback).
4) Meeting copilot: live transcription, auto-notes, action items, follow-up emails.
5) Automation engine: triggers for time, “no reply”, calendar changes; background jobs with retries and notifications.
6) Generic http_request + secrets_store to quickly add new services before building native adapters.
7) OS-level primitives for basic desktop automation and screenshot understanding.
8) India-first transactions: UPI payments + Maps ETA + Ola/Uber + Swiggy/Zomato.

Example user flows unlocked by the above
- “If my email to Priya isn’t answered in 48 hours, ping her on Slack, CC my manager, and resurface the thread in my daily brief.”
- “Join my 3:30 pm Zoom, live-transcribe, capture decisions and owner-next steps, and file a Jira ticket for each action.”
- “Pay the BESCOM bill via UPI, save the receipt in Drive, and update my expense sheet.”
- “Book the 7:10 pm KIAL Volvo from Indiranagar; if the ETA to the airport exceeds 90 minutes, request an Ola Prime instead.”

Architecture and safety guardrails to include
- Tool registry with health checks, rate limits, and backoff policies.
- Risk scoring for actions; auto-approval below threshold, user approval above.
- Dry-run preview for destructive or transactional actions.
- Per-connector OAuth with scoped tokens; secrets vaulted; full audit log of every tool call.

KPIs to track
- Task completion rate without human correction
- First-response and end-to-end completion latency
- Autonomy ratio (tasks completed without prompts/clarifications)
- Safety incidents and approval deflections
- User retention and weekly active power tasks per user

What I can do next for you
- Draft JSON schemas for the top 8 functions above (memory, http_request, retrieval, automation, approvals, slack/whatsapp, transcription, payments_upi).
- Propose PRDs and user stories for “Meeting Copilot,” “Follow-up Nudge,” and “Daily Brief.”
- Help design the permission/approval UX and the audit log taxonomy.

If you share which user segment you want to delight first (solo pros, founders, sales, engineers, ops) and your preferred initial channels (desktop, mobile, voice), I’ll tailor the exact function list and rollout plan.
